pki.path.validate.reason: The RFC 5280 §6 path-validation surface is broad and still hardening, and it is not yet interop-proven against an independent path validator in the integration harness. Graduate once the verify surface has stabilized and an external validator cross-checks a path it accepts/rejects.

pki.path.crlChecker.reason: Rides the same SIG_ALGS verify bridge as pki.path.validate and the same not-yet-interop-proven revocation path. Graduate alongside pki.path.validate.

pki.schema.attrcert.parse.reason: The RFC 5755 attribute-certificate parser is not yet interop-proven — OpenSSL cannot emit RFC 5755 attribute certificates, so no independent oracle round-trips a structure it decodes — and its return shape is not frozen. It is part of the attribute-cert / CRMF / CMP format cohort the schema.all and schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an independent implementation validates a structure it decodes.

pki.schema.attrcert.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same attribute-certificate parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no independent oracle emits RFC 5755 attribute certificates). Graduate alongside pki.schema.attrcert.parse.

pki.schema.attrcert.pemEncode.reason: Rides the same attribute-certificate parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no independent oracle emits RFC 5755 attribute certificates). Graduate alongside pki.schema.attrcert.parse.

pki.schema.all.reason: The format enumerator routes to the still-experimental attribute-cert, CRMF, and CMP parsers; marking it stable would freeze their return shapes and continued registration into a stable contract. Graduate once every routed format is stable.

pki.schema.parse.reason: The auto-routing dispatcher's stable contract would include the still-experimental attribute-cert, CRMF, and CMP return shapes it routes to, so an allowed change to any of those would break callers of a stable schema.parse. Graduate once every routed format is stable.

pki.schema.crmf.parse.reason: The RFC 4211 CertReqMessages parser is not yet interop-proven against an independent CRMF/CMP implementation in the integration harness (OpenSSL has no offline CRMF generator), so its return shape is not frozen. Part of the same format cohort schema.all / schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an external implementation validates a request it decodes.

pki.schema.crmf.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same CRMF parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no cross-implementation interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.crmf.parse.

pki.schema.crmf.pemEncode.reason: Rides the same CRMF parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no cross-implementation interop round-trip in the harness). Graduate alongside pki.schema.crmf.parse.

pki.schema.cmp.parse.reason: The RFC 9810 PKIMessage parser is not yet interop-proven — OpenSSL's cmp subcommand is client-only and needs a live server as the oracle peer, so no offline cross-check round-trips a message it decodes — and its 27-arm body return shape is not frozen. Part of the format cohort schema.all / schema.parse graduation waits on. Graduate once an independent implementation validates a message it decodes.

pki.schema.cmp.pemDecode.reason: Rides the same CMP parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no offline cross-implementation oracle). Graduate alongside pki.schema.cmp.parse.

pki.schema.cmp.pemEncode.reason: Rides the same CMP parser whose return shape is not yet frozen (no offline cross-implementation oracle). Graduate alongside pki.schema.cmp.parse.

pki.schema.engine.encode.reason: The schema engine's encode direction backs the round-trip tests but has no external consumer that pins its API; the combinator surface is still absorbing new leaves. Graduate once the combinator set is settled and an operator-facing encode path depends on it.

pki.schema.engine.embeddedDer.reason: The embedded-DER leaf is part of the still-settling engine combinator surface; it has no external consumer pinning its return shape. Graduate alongside pki.schema.engine.encode once the leaf set is settled.

pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.reason: An OpenSSL `pkcs12 -export` round-trip now covers this parser in the integration harness, so its interop blocker is cleared; graduation is held for a deliberate batched stabilization pass with the rest of the format cohort (attribute-cert, CRMF, CMP) so schema.all / schema.parse can graduate together rather than piecemeal.

pki.schema.pkcs12.pemDecode.reason: Rides the pkcs12 parser held for the batched format-cohort stabilization pass. Graduate alongside pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.

pki.schema.pkcs12.pemEncode.reason: Rides the pkcs12 parser held for the batched format-cohort stabilization pass. Graduate alongside pki.schema.pkcs12.parse.

pki.ct.parseSctList.reason: The RFC 6962 signed-certificate-timestamp list parser is not yet interop-proven end-to-end: no test in the harness verifies an SCT it decodes against a production CT log's public key, so its return shape is not frozen. Graduate once an independent verifier cross-checks an SCT it parses.

pki.ct.reconstructSignedData.reason: Reconstructs the RFC 6962 digitally-signed structure that an SCT signature covers; it is unverified against an independent CT verifier end-to-end, the same interop gap as pki.ct.parseSctList. Graduate alongside pki.ct.parseSctList.

pki.schema.smime.parseSigningCertificate.reason: The RFC 5035 / RFC 8551 S/MIME ESS SigningCertificate decoder is not yet interop-proven: the openssl-cms oracle for the ESS signing-certificate attribute is available but no fixtures row is registered in the integration harness yet (auto-interop.test.js flags exactly this), so no independent implementation round-trips a structure it decodes. Graduate once an interop fixtures row exercises an ESS attribute through OpenSSL.

The RFC 5035 ESS SigningCertificateV2 decoder rides the same not-yet-registered ESS interop fixtures row as pki.schema.smime.parseSigningCertificate. Graduate alongside it once the OpenSSL round-trip lands.

pki.schema.smime.parseSmimeCapabilities.reason: The RFC 8551 SMIMECapabilities decoder's return shape is part of the S/MIME ESS attribute cohort that is not yet independently interop-verified (no OpenSSL/NSS round-trip of the attribute in the harness). Graduate alongside the ESS interop fixtures row.

pki.schema.smime.decodeAttribute.reason: The OID-dispatch entry that routes a CMS attribute to the ESS / SMIMECapabilities decoders; its return shape is frozen only once those decoders are interop-proven. Graduate alongside the ESS interop fixtures row.
